text:
It’s not unusual for millions to be banked by the winner of golf’s biggest tournaments but only £50,000 will go the victor at Le Golf National just outsideParison Sunday.

The gold medal hung around the neck of the Olympic champion will mean just as much as winning one of the four majors, a feat only currently experienced by Xander Schauffele, who triumphed in Tokyo three years ago while winning both theUSPGA at Pinehurst and the Open at Royal Troon in the past month.

The Albatross Course, with its long fairways, fast greens and tricky bunkers was the venue for Europe’s remarkable victory in 2018 when they regained the Ryder Cup from the United States.

Will it be the course always remembered in Ireland as the place where Shane Lowry powered his way to Olympic gold?

He is fresh from finishing a solo sixth at Troon and after speaking of his pride at carrying his nation’s flag at the opening ceremony on Friday, it would be one of those fairytale stories which light up the Olympics if he topped the leaderboard on Sunday evening.

It won’t be easy as the likes of Scottie Scheffler, Jon Rahm, Rory McIlroy, Ludvig Aberg and Hideki Matsuyama are also representing their countries.

But Lowry is no stranger to handling the biggest of occasions and won the Zurich Classic with Big Mac earlier this year while enjoying four top-ten finishes with a tied sixth at the US PGA and third at the Arnold Palmer Invitational.

After being the toast Ireland when he won the 2019 Open at Royal Portrush in County Antrim, they will be raising a glass to Lowry all over the Emerald Isle when he has a gold medal placed round his neck which is 28/1 with Ladbrokes.

Matsuyama at 25/1 with bet365 and Sweden’s Alex Noren at 40/1 with Ladbrokes are our other each-way bets.

• Dominic Calvert-Lewin is 10/11 with Sky Bet to join Newcastle and the Everton striker is likely to replace Callum Wilson, who is 9/2 with William Hill to move to Brentford.

• Tottenham are the 3/1 favourites with Sky Bet to sign Juventus star Federico Chiesa before the end of the transfer window.

title: Shane Lowry can take the Olympic title away from Xander Schauffele | Metro News
description: There’s tough competition from golf’s biggest names by Shane Lowry can stand tallest on the winner’s podium in Paris.
keywords: Sport,Brentford FC,Juventus FC,Metro newspaper,Newcastle United,Olympics,Paris,Rory McIlroy,Scottie Scheffler,Simone Biles,The Tipster,Tottenham Hotspur
